exemplar.title: What is the effect of different internal air pressures (5,8,11 psi) of a basketball on its rebound height when dropped from an initial height of one meter?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the effect of different internal air pressures (5,8,11 psi) of a basketball on its rebound height when dropped from an initial height of one meter?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables, graphs and fitted lines (with RÂ²) are presented clearly, with units and uncertainties noted.
Processing steps (mean calculations, COR derivation) are carried out accurately with appropriate statistical tools.
Communication of data recording and processing is generally both clear and precise, facilitating reader understanding.
weaknesses: Uncertainties are not propagated through the COR calculation, omitting error bounds on key results.
Significant figures in results exceed instrument precision, and the definition of the COR equation is incomplete in places.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication and processing are essentially clear and precise (band-5) and the analysis is carried out appropriately (band-5). Consideration of uncertainties is present but incomplete (band-4). On balance, these strengths outweigh the omissions, meriting 5/6.

strengths: The research question is situated in a highly specific context (basketball performance under NBA pressure guidelines).
Methodology for data collection is described step-by-step with clear equipment lists, enabling reproducibility.
Independent, dependent, and controlled variables are well defined and justified, showing thoughtful experimental control.
weaknesses: The choice of only three trials per condition is not justified; sampling robustness is unaddressed.
Exact camera distance and lighting conditions are not specified, leaving minor reproducibility gaps.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands comfortably meet the top band descriptors for context and methodological detail (both rated band-5). The third strand (methodological considerations) is solidly 'described' but lacks depth on sampling rationale, placing it at band-4. Overall, a holistic score of 5/6 reflects excellent design with only minor omissions preventing full marks.

strengths: The conclusion explicitly answers the research question, citing numerical trends and RÂ² values to support claims.
It links back to the original hypothesis and highlights practical significance (e.g., 0.14 m difference in live play).
weaknesses: Comparison to accepted scientific context is superficial; no quantitative benchmarks from literature are provided.
Stated inability to find literature values limits the depth of scientific context.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with and justified by the analysis (band-5), but the scientificâcontext comparison is only superficial (band-1â2). This combination places the criterion in band-3â4; a score of 4/6 appropriately reflects a strong conclusion held back by limited external benchmarking.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (human release error, camera angle, gauge uncertainty) are clearly described.
Relevant and realistic improvements (automated release, better tracking, premium gauge) are suggested, showing insight into practical fixes.
weaknesses: The relative impact of each weakness on overall uncertainty is not quantified (no % error contributions).
Improvements lack explanation of expected error reduction magnitude or feasibility analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Weaknesses are described with specificity (band-4), and improvements are realistic and relevant (band-3). However, neither the relative impact nor the quantified benefits of suggestions are explained, anchoring the holistic score at 3/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student frames the question within the very specific context of basketball performance, citing NBA pressure regulations and explaining how bounce affects dribbling and shooting consistency.  This moves far beyond a generic statement and shows a clear, sport-specific rationale, so the descriptor for a âspecific and appropriate contextâ is met.  Minor narrative digressions prevent it from feeling textbook-perfect, hence 5 rather than 6.

Instrument uncertainties are quoted, range/2 is used for random error and error bars are plotted on the rebound-height graph.  No uncertainty is propagated into COR, and discussion of systematic errors is qualitative only.  Hence uncertainties are âconsidered with some significant omissionsâ, matching band 3â4; 4 is given for the generally consistent if incomplete treatment.

Relevant variables are identified and each is justified; the choice of three pressures and three trials is rationalised from preliminary testing.  However, an explanation of why only three data points are sufficient to reveal the underlying relationship is absent, and there is limited consideration of sampling size or range.  This satisfies the âdescribedâ qualifier of band 3â4, so 4 marks are appropriate.

Specific weaknessesâhuman release error, tracking inaccuracy, camera angle, gauge uncertaintyâare described, and their nature (random vs systematic) is recognised.  The relative impact on accuracy or precision is hinted at but not quantitatively analysed.  This meets the descriptor for âdescribes specific weaknessesâ; hence 4 marks in band 3â4.

Step-by-step instructions, equipment list (with precisions) and camera alignment guidance allow another investigator to reproduce the data-collection method reliably.  Only small omissions (e.g. exact camera distance, lighting) remain, so reproduction is clearly possible; this aligns with the top band, but the minor gaps justify 5 rather than the full 6.

Means, uncertainties, COR calculation and linear fits are carried out correctly; no arithmetic errors are evident.  The choice of statistical tools is appropriate to the simple data set.  The small sample size slightly limits robustness, so a score of 5 (solidly âappropriate and accurateâ but not exemplary) is justified.

For each weakness, a realistic improvement (release mechanism, automated tracking, tripod, premium gauge) is suggested and briefly justified.  The explanations are functional but not deeply developed (no discussion of expected error reduction magnitude), fitting the âdescribedâ level.  The suggestions are relevant and attainable, so 3 marks are appropriate within band 3â4.

Data tables are neatly formatted with units and uncertainties; processed tables, graphs with labelled axes, fitted lines and RÂ² values are provided.  Presentation is clear and largely precise, though the height uncertainty of Â±0.0005 m is unrealistically small, showing slight imprecision in reporting.  Thus the work meets the âclear and preciseâ descriptor but not perfectly, warranting 5 marks.

Comparison to accepted scientific context is limited to noting NBA pressure guidelines; the student even states an inability to find literature values.  This is a superficial comparison and does not quantitatively benchmark COR or rebound height against published data.  Therefore the work fits the 1â2 band; 2 marks are awarded for at least some attempt.

The conclusion directly answers the research question, cites numerical trends and RÂ² values, and links back to the hypothesis.  It is fully consistent with the analysis; however, the depth of quantitative reference (e.g. no confidence intervals) could be richer, so 5 rather than 6 marks are appropriate.

annotations.comment: Uncertainties in rebound height should be propagated through the COR calculation using $$ rac{\\Delta h_f/h_i}{2\\sqrt{h_f/h_i}}$$ to report COR error bounds.
Instead of only describing the caption, include the actual raw data plot here (axes and data points) to support visual analysis.
The mean formula is correctly stated, but reporting results to four decimal places ignores instrument resolution; align significant figures with measurement precision.
Reorganize the evaluation section to quantify the relative impact of each error source (e.g., % error contribution) rather than listing qualitatively.
Strengths are clearly articulated with detailed explanations, providing a solid reflection on methodological choices.
The student effectively grounds the research question in the specific context of basketball performance, linking internal pressure variations to dribbling and shooting consistency.
The explanation of pressure using P = F/A is overly simplistic and neglects the underlying gas laws; consider referencing the ideal gas law to clarify how air volume and temperature interact.
Explicitly introduce the formula $$	ext{COR}= rac{h_{	ext{final}}}{h_{	ext{initial}}}$$ (or its squareâroot form) in the background to strengthen conceptual clarity.
Footnote citations are inconsistently formatted, which can obscure source credibility; adopt a uniform style (e.g., APA or MLA) throughout.

The equation âCOR = Î³â is incomplete and lacks definition of Î³; clarify and use the correct form $$	ext{COR}=  rac{h_{	ext{final}}}{h_{	ext{initial}}}$$ or $$	ext{COR}= igl(h_f/h_i igr)^{1/2}$$.
The deformation photo (figure 1) lacks a scale bar or reference object, limiting quantitative interpretation of bounce deformation.
Variables are clearly defined and controlled variables are well justified, enhancing reproducibility of the design.
Justification of the trial number (n=3) is absent, leaving sampling robustness unaddressed; explain why three repeats sufficiently capture variability.
In step 2 of the procedure, specify the horizontal distance between camera and ball to ensure consistent field of view for reproducibility.
Limiting each condition to only three trials reduces statistical confidence; consider increasing repeats to 5â7 trials.
The raw rebound height uncertainty (Â±0.0005 m) is unrealistically small relative to camera and tracking resolution; revise to reflect instrument precision.
Averaged rebound heights in Table 7 carry inherited instrument uncertaintyâconsider recalculating their uncertainties via standard error of the mean.
Graphs (figure 7) should include error bars on each data point to reflect measurement uncertainties and improve data communication.
Linear fits need reported uncertainties in slope and intercept alongside RÂ² for a complete trend analysis.
The conclusion succinctly answers the research question with reference to numerical trends and RÂ² values, fully linking back to the hypothesis.
The practical significance of a 0.14 m rebound-height difference is well noted, tying lab data to real gameplay.
Comparison to scientific literature remains superficial; include quantitative benchmarks of COR from published studies for a deeper context.
Improvement suggestions lack quantification of expected benefits or feasibility analysis; estimate the magnitude of error reduction for each device.
For the mounting bracket improvement, calculate how much systematic error would be reduced to justify resource allocation.
The extension proposing investigation of temperature effects on COR is relevant and would yield valuable insight into real playing conditions.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
